name: Empty Mountains – circular by Valdelinares from Virgen de la Vega via Linares de la Mora
name_original: Montañas Vacías – circular por Valdelinares desde Virgen de la Vega por Linares de la Mora
region_intro: Touring cycling around Alcalá De La Selva offers routes through the Sierra de Gúdar mountains, characterized by varied terrain and natural features. The region features lush forests, clear rivers like the Alcalá River, and numerous springs, with elevations reaching over 1400 meters. Cyclists can navigate diverse landscapes, from serene valleys to mountain slopes, providing a range of challenges and scenic views. The area's natural beauty and varied topography make it suitable for multi-day touring cycling routes. Best touring cycling routes around Alcalá De La Selva The most popular touring cycling route is Empty Mountains – circular by Valdelinares from Virgen de la Vega via Linares de la Mora, a 23.5 miles (37.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountain landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Linares de Mora – Virgen de la Vega loop from Bandera, a difficult 38.4 miles (61.9 km) path. This route traverses mountainous terrain with substantial climbs and panoramic views. Local touring cyclists also love the Virgen de la Vega – Puerto de Valdelinares loop from Urbanización Solano de la Vega, a 27.9 miles (44.9 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Gúdar mountains, often completed in about 3 hours 12 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcalá De La Selva is defined by mountain scenery, lush forests, and varied elevation. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes are classified as difficult. The routes in Alcalá De La Selva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcalá De La Selva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Alcalá De La Selva?
A: There are 16 touring cycling routes around Alcalá De La Selva. Most of these routes are classified as difficult, offering significant elevation gain through mountainous landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Alcalá De La Selva?
A: You can expect varied terrain, primarily through the Sierra de Gúdar mountains. The routes feature lush forests, clear rivers like the Alcalá River, and numerous springs. Elevations can reach over 1400 meters, providing a mix of serene valleys and challenging mountain slopes.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes in Alcalá De La Selva for beginners or families?
A: While many routes in Alcalá De La Selva are challenging, there are 2 moderate touring cycling routes available. The region's mountainous character means most paths involve significant climbs, so even moderate routes may require some cycling experience.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I visit along the touring cycling routes?
A: You can incorporate visits to several historical and cultural sites. Consider stopping at the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5359402" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de la Selva Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the 18th-century Santuario de la Virgen de la Vega, or the large Gothic-Renaissance Iglesia de San Simón y San Judas. The historic town center itself also offers a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: Are there any natural landmarks or scenic viewpoints to see while cycling?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural beauty. You can find impressive views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5805644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Summit and Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt;, explore the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2381633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Ojerada Rock Formation&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the tranquil Barranco del Nacimiento. The Alcalá River also features the beautiful Cascada del Molino and Cascada de la Hiedra.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Alcalá De La Selva?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For example, you can try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955765456/empty-mountains-circular-by-valdelinares-from-virgen-de-la-vega-via-linares-de-la-mora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Empty Mountains – circular by Valdelinares from Virgen de la Vega via Linares de la Mora&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de la Vega – Puerto de Valdelinares loop from Urbanización Solano de la Vega&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What makes Alcalá De La Selva a good destination for touring cyclists?
A: Alcalá De La Selva is an excellent destination for touring cyclists due to its stunning natural landscapes, varied and challenging terrain within the Sierra de Gúdar mountains, and tranquil rural charm. It's part of the 'Montañas Vacías' (Empty Mountains) bikepacking route, known for its less-trafficked roads and opportunities for adventure and solitude.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Alcalá De La Selva?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the stunning mountain scenery, the quiet roads, and the sense of adventure found in this 'empty Spain' region.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. 14 of the 16 touring cycling routes in Alcalá De La Selva are classified as difficult. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Linares de Mora – Virgen de la Vega loop from Bandera&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 61.9 km with over 1400 meters of elevation gain, offer substantial challenges for experienced riders.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alcalá De La Selva?
A: The mountainous climate means spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the heat of summer and the colder, potentially snowy conditions of winter at higher elevations. These seasons also showcase the region's lush forests at their best.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Valdelinares Ski Resort?
A: Yes, there is a route that includes the Valdelinares Ski Resort. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de la Vega – Valdelinares Ski Resort loop from Ermita Santuario de la Virgen de la Vega&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 21.6 km trail.

Q: Can I find routes that connect to other notable castles in the area?
A: Yes, you can find routes that extend to other historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31517948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de la Vega – Mora de Rubielos Castle loop from Ermita Santuario de la Virgen de la Vega&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 60.7 km route that takes you to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5113901" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mora de Rubielos Castle&lt;/a&gt;.
